question: The Union Cabinet has approved the extension of the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) until which year, and with what enhanced total outlay?
rationale: According to the PDF (Page 6), the Union Cabinet approved the extension of the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) until 2028 with an enhanced total outlay of Rs 8.69 lakh crore.

question: Which of the following countries is NOT covered under the amended Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) rules governing investments from Land Border Countries (LBCs)?
rationale: The amended FDI rules apply to countries sharing a land border with India, specifically: China, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Nepal, Bhutan, Myanmar, and Afghanistan. Sri Lanka does not share a land border with India. (Page 6)

question:
Match the following infrastructure projects approved by the Cabinet in March 2026 with their respective states:

Column I:
1. Madurai Airport International Status
2. Rs. 4,474 crore railway multitracking projects
3. Rs. 3,839 crore highway widening project

Column II:
A. Madhya Pradesh
B. Tamil Nadu
C. West Bengal and Jharkhand

Select the correct answer:
rationale: Madurai Airport is in Tamil Nadu (1-B); Railway multitracking projects are in West Bengal and Jharkhand (2-C); Highway widening project is in Madhya Pradesh (3-A). (Page 6)

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'BHAVYA scheme':

1. It has a total financial outlay of Rs 33,660 crore.
2. It aims to develop 100 plug-and-play industrial parks across the country.
3. The government will provide up to Rs 5 crore per acre for infrastructure development.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. Statement 3 is incorrect because the GoI will provide up to Rs 1 crore per acre (not Rs 5 crore) for core, value-added, and social infrastructure. (Page 6)

question: Under the newly approved 'Small Hydro Power (SHP) Development Scheme', what is the maximum limit of central financial assistance provided per project for North East states and international border districts?
rationale: Under the SHP Development scheme, central financial assistance is Rs 3.6 crore per MW or 30% of the project cost, whichever is lower, with an upper limit of Rs 30 crore per project for NE states and international border districts. (Page 7)

question: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA) approved Rs 1,718.56 crore as Minimum Support Price (MSP) funding for which crop's farmers?
rationale: The CCEA approved MSP funding of Rs 1,718.56 crore to the Cotton Corporation of India Limited (CCI) for the 2023-24 cotton season. (Page 7)

question: Which of the following statements about the Revamped UDAN Scheme is NOT correct?

options.A: It aims to strengthen regional air connectivity.
options.B: It targets economic growth in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
options.C: It will be implemented over a 5-year period from FY26-27 to FY31.
options.D: The total financial outlay of the scheme is Rs 28,840 crore.
options.E: The scheme targets underserved and unserved areas.

rationale: The revamped UDAN scheme will be implemented over a 10-year period from Financial Year 2026-27 (FY27) to FY36, not a 5-year period. (Page 7)

question: The Immigration, Visa, Foreigners Registration & Tracking (IVFRT) Scheme has been extended until which date?
rationale: The IVFRT Scheme has been extended for another five years, from April 01, 2026, to March 31, 2031, with a budget outlay of Rs 1,800 crore. (Page 7)

question: According to India’s approved Nationally Determined Contribution (NDC) targets for 2031-35, the country aims to achieve what percentage of installed electric power capacity from non-fossil fuel resources by 2035?
rationale: India aims to achieve 60% of installed electric power capacity from non-fossil fuel resources by 2035, up from the 52.57% capacity reached by February 2026. (Page 8)

question:
Match the following tribal festivals announced by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs (MoTA) with their respective venues:

Column I:
1. Tribes Art Fest (TAF) 2026
2. Living Roots Festival (LRF)
3. Bharat Tribes Fest (BTF) 2026

Column II:
A. Sunder Nursery, New Delhi
B. Travancore Palace Art Gallery, New Delhi
C. Bikaner House and India Gate, New Delhi

Select the correct answer:
rationale: Tribes Art Fest 2026 will be held at Travancore Palace Art Gallery (1-B); Living Roots Festival will take place at Bikaner House and India Gate (2-C); Bharat Tribes Fest 2026 is scheduled at Sunder Nursery (3-A). (Page 8)

question: The Deputy Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh, Pawan Kalyan, launched 'Project HANUMAN' on World Wildlife Day. What does 'HANUMAN' stand for in this context?

options.A: Habitat and Nature Units for Monitoring, Aid and Nursing of Wildlife
options.B: Healing and Nurturing Units for Monitoring, Aid and Nursing of Wildlife
options.C: Human and Nature Unified Management, Aid and Nursing
options.D: Healing and Nature Units for Managing, Aiding and Nurturing Wildlife
options.E: Habitat and Nurturing Units for Monitoring, Aid and Nursing of Wildlife

rationale: Project HANUMAN stands for 'Healing and Nurturing Units for Monitoring, Aid and Nursing of Wildlife', launched to address the growing issue of human-wildlife conflict. (Page 8)

question:
Arrange the following states in descending order based on the total outlay of their state budgets presented for FY26-27:

1. Maharashtra
2. Karnataka
3. Telangana
4. Punjab

Select the correct answer:
rationale: Maharashtra (Rs 7.69 lakh crore), Karnataka (Rs 4.48 lakh crore), Telangana (Rs 3.24 lakh crore), Punjab (Rs 2.60 lakh crore). The correct descending order is 1, 2, 3, 4. (Pages 10-11)

question: Gujarat became the second state in India to pass the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill. What is the penalty specified in the Gujarat UCC 2026 for failing to register a live-in relationship within 1 month?

options.A: Maximum 1 month imprisonment or a fine of Rs 5,000
options.B: Maximum 6 months imprisonment or a fine of Rs 25,000
options.C: Maximum 3 months imprisonment or a fine of Rs 10,000
options.D: Maximum 1 year imprisonment or a fine of Rs 50,000

rationale: The law specifies that failure to register a live-in relationship within 1 month could attract a maximum of 3 months of imprisonment or a fine of Rs 10,000. (Page 9)

question: The Government of Himachal Pradesh signed three MoUs with which organization to strengthen the state's dairy sector and improve milk processing capacity?

options.C: Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI)

rationale: The Government of Himachal Pradesh signed three Memorandums of Understanding (MoU) with the National Dairy Development Board (NDDB) to enhance the state's dairy sector. (Page 10)

question:
Assertion (A): Meghalaya became the 12th state in the country to sign a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on reforms under JJM 2.0.
Reason (R): JJM 2.0 aims to completely shift the focus from sustainable service delivery back to basic infrastructure creation.

Choose the correct option:

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.
options.C: A is true but R is false.
options.D: A is false but R is true.
options.E: Both A and R are false.

rationale: Assertion (A) is true as Meghalaya became the 12th state to sign the MoU under JJM 2.0. Reason (R) is false because JJM 2.0 shifts focus FROM infrastructure creation TO sustainable service delivery and drinking water governance, not the other way around. (Pages 6, 10)

question: Tamil Nadu inaugurated the state's first Dark Sky Park to promote astro-tourism. Where is this park located?

options.B: Ariyur Shola Reserve Forest, Kolli Hills
options.D: Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park

rationale: Tamil Nadu inaugurated its first Dark Sky Park at Ariyur Shola Reserve Forest, Kolli Hills, Namakkal district. (Page 11)

question: Who has been appointed as the new Chief Justice of the Madras High Court, succeeding Justice Manindra Mohan Shrivastava?
rationale: President Droupadi Murmu appointed Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari as the Chief Justice of the Madras High Court. (Page 11)

question:
Match the following State Government initiatives with their respective states:

Column I:
1. Project HANUMAN
2. Gaudham Yojana
3. Nivesh Mitra 3.0

Column II:
A. Uttar Pradesh
B. Chhattisgarh
C. Andhra Pradesh

Select the correct answer:
rationale: Project HANUMAN is in Andhra Pradesh (1-C); Gaudham Yojana is in Chhattisgarh (2-B); Nivesh Mitra 3.0 is in Uttar Pradesh (3-A). (Pages 8, 9, 12)

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'Saksham Skill Census':

1. It is India’s first AI-powered skill census.
2. It was formally launched by the Government of Karnataka.
3. The survey aims to evaluate the skills of youth aged 18-40 years.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: Statements 1 and 3 are correct. Statement 2 is incorrect because the 'Saksham Skill Census' was launched by Ashish Shelar from the Government of Maharashtra, not Karnataka. (Page 12)

question: Which of the following initiatives was NOT launched by Controller General of Accounts (CGA) T.C.A. Kalyani to improve the governance of banks handling government transactions?

options.D: None of the above (Both A and B were launched)

rationale: CGA T.C.A. Kalyani launched two major initiatives: the Government Bank Dashboard and the Government Bank Manual. The 'Government Bank Transaction Portal' is not mentioned. (Page 12)

question:
Assertion (A): Union Minister Amit Shah launched two mascots named 'Pragati' and 'Vikas' for Census-2027.
Reason (R): The mascots represent the equal participation of women and men in achieving India’s national mission of becoming a developed nation by 2047.

Choose the correct option:

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.
options.C: A is true but R is false.
options.D: A is false but R is true.
options.E: Both A and R are false.

rationale: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and the Reason correctly explains the significance of having one female mascot (Pragati) and one male mascot (Vikas) for Census-2027. (Page 13)

question: International Business Machines Corporation (IBM) launched its first Sangam Infrastructure Innovation Center in which Indian city?
rationale: IBM launched its first Sangam Infrastructure Innovation Center at the India Systems Development Lab (ISDL) campus in Bengaluru, Karnataka. (Page 13)

question: The newly launched premium express service '24 Speed Post' by India Post guarantees next-day (D+1) delivery. Which of the following cities is NOT part of the initial phase covering six major metro cities?
rationale: The initial phase of '24 Speed Post' covers New Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata, Bengaluru, and Hyderabad. Pune is not included. (Page 14)

question: The 'PRARAMBH 2026' campaign, launched by Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman, is primarily focused on creating awareness about the implementation of:
rationale: PRARAMBH 2026 is a nationwide Income Tax Department awareness campaign for the implementation of the Income Tax (IT) Act, 2025, effective from April 1, 2026. (Page 14)

question:
Match the following digital initiatives with their respective ministries:

Column I:
1. 5G Innovation Hackathon 2026
2. PRISM-SG Portal
3. Internal Audit Portal

Column II:
A. Ministry of Rural Development
B. Ministry of Communications
C. Ministry of Road Transport and Highways & Ministry of Railways

Select the correct answer:
rationale: 5G Innovation Hackathon is under DoT (Ministry of Communications) [1-B]; PRISM-SG Portal is a joint launch by MoRTH and MoR [2-C]; Internal Audit Portal is under Ministry of Rural Development [3-A]. (Pages 15-16)

question: Which of the following projects was NOT inaugurated on National Waterway-2 (NW-2) in Dibrugarh, Assam, by Union Minister Sarbananda Sonowal?
rationale: The three major projects inaugurated were the Bogibeel Complex, Dhubri Gateway, and the Heritage Building. The 'Pandu Terminal Upgrade' was not among these specific inaugurations, though Pandu is a site for a riverine lighthouse mentioned elsewhere. (Page 16)

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Phase I of Noida International Airport (NIA):

1. It was developed at a cost of Rs 11,200 crore under the PPP model.
2. It will serve as the second international gateway for the Delhi NCR region.
3. The airport was built in collaboration with Frankfurt Airport, Germany.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. Statement 3 is incorrect because the airport was built in collaboration with Zurich Airport International AG, Switzerland, not Frankfurt Airport. (Page 17)

question: The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare (MoAFW) announced two landmark legislative reforms in Mussoorie, Uttarakhand. Under the New Seed Act 2026, what is the maximum fine for deliberate offences involving substandard products?
rationale: The New Seed Act 2026 introduces mandatory registration and QR codes on seed packets, and fines have been increased to a maximum of Rs 30 lakh, accompanied by possible imprisonment for deliberate offences. (Page 18)

question:
Arrange the following lighthouses, where foundation stones were laid to form India's first riverine lighthouse infrastructure on National Waterway-2, geographically from West to East along the Brahmaputra River:

Note: Rely on general awareness or the PDF grouping (Bogibeel, Pandu, Silghat, Biswanath Ghat).
Which of the following is the correct list of locations as mentioned in the document?
rationale: The four river lighthouses will be developed at Bogibeel, Pandu, Silghat, and Biswanath Ghat along the Brahmaputra on National Waterway-2 (NW-2). (Page 19)

question:
Match the following newly appointed Governors (March 2026) with their respective States/UTs:

Column I:
1. Lt Gen Syed Ata Hasnain (Retd)
2. Jishnu Dev Varma
3. Nand Kishore Yadav
4. Taranjit Singh Sandhu

Column II:
A. Maharashtra
B. Bihar
C. Delhi
D. Nagaland

Select the correct answer:
rationale: Lt Gen Syed Ata Hasnain (Retd) - Bihar (1-B); Jishnu Dev Varma - Maharashtra (2-A); Nand Kishore Yadav - Nagaland (3-D); Taranjit Singh Sandhu - Delhi (4-C). (Page 20)

question: The 6th Phase of Mandatory Gold Hallmarking covers how many districts across India?
rationale: The sixth phase (Phase VI) of the mandatory hallmarking added 7 more districts, bringing the total to 380 districts. (Page 22)

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the new standards for Green Ammonia and Green Methanol notified by the Government of India:

1. Green Ammonia must have total non-biogenic greenhouse gas emissions of not more than 0.38 kg CO2 eq per kg.
2. Green Methanol must have lifecycle emissions of not more than 0.44 kg CO2 eq per kg.
3. The standards were notified by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC).

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. Statement 3 is incorrect because the standards were notified by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE), not MoEFCC. (Page 21)

question: Which country recently ratified the Framework Agreement of the International Solar Alliance (ISA), formally joining as the 118th member country?
rationale: Sena Latif, Ambassador of Romania, submitted the Instrument of Ratification, formally confirming Romania's ratification of the Framework Agreement of the ISA. (Page 21)

question: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) expanded the ALMM framework to include Solar Ingots and Wafers under ALMM List-III. From which date will this be effective?
rationale: The MNRE extended the ALMM framework by introducing ALMM List-III for solar ingots and wafers, effective from 1 June 2028. (Page 24)

question: The Geological Survey of India (GSI) declared the hill area surrounding Kalinjar Fort as a National Geo-Heritage Site. In which state is Kalinjar Fort located?
rationale: The GSI declared the hill area surrounding Kalinjar Fort in Banda district, Uttar Pradesh (UP) as a National Geo-Heritage Site. (Page 24)

question: The 'Metropolis' project by Reliance MET City became the first project in India to receive the LEED Platinum Pre-Certification. Where is this project located?
rationale: Metropolis, the flagship commercial project of Reliance MET City in Jhajjar (Haryana), became the first project in India to receive LEED Platinum Pre-Certification. (Page 24)

question:
Assertion (A): The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW) constituted the National Dental Commission (NDC) in March 2026.
Reason (R): The NDC has been established to replace the Medical Council of India (MCI) and align dental education with global standards.

Choose the correct option:

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.
options.C: A is true but R is false.
options.D: A is false but R is true.
options.E: Both A and R are false.

rationale: Assertion (A) is true as MoHFW constituted the NDC. Reason (R) is false because the NDC replaces the Dental Council of India (DCI) and repeals the Dentist Act, 1948, not the Medical Council of India (MCI). (Page 25)

question: The Government of India introduced the Credit Guarantee Scheme for Microfinance Institutions 2.0 (CGSMFI-2.0). What is the maximum loan guarantee amount issued under this scheme, or until when is it valid?
rationale: The scheme will be valid till 30.06.2026 or till loan guarantees of Rs 20,000 crores are issued, whichever is earlier. (Page 25)

question: India's first hydrogen-powered train completed its successful oscillation trials. Between which two stations will this train run?
rationale: India's first hydrogen-powered train will run between Jind and Sonipat in Haryana. (Page 26)

question: Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited (APSEZ) operationalised India's first Port of Refuge (PoR). Which two sites have been designated as PoR?
rationale: APSEZ designated two sites as PoR: Dighi Port on the west coast and Gopalpur Port on the east coast. (Page 26)

question: President Droupadi Murmu dedicated four welfare schemes for women in New Delhi. Which of the following is NOT one of these four schemes?

options.D: Free Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) Cylinder Scheme

rationale: The four schemes are Delhi Lakhpati Bitiya Scheme, Saheli Pink Smart Card, Meri Poonji Mera Adhikaar, and the Free Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) Cylinder Scheme. 'Nari Shakti Samriddh Yojana' is not among them. (Page 26)

question: The Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) launched the RELIEF scheme to support Indian exporters impacted by the conflict in West Asia. What does 'RELIEF' stand for?

options.A: Resilience and Logistics Intervention for Export Facilitation
options.B: Reimbursement and Logistics Intervention for Export Finance
options.C: Resilience and Liquidity Intervention for Export Facilitation
options.D: Relief and Logistics Initiative for Export Facilitation
options.E: Resilience and Logistics Initiative for Economic Facilitation

rationale: RELIEF stands for Resilience and Logistics Intervention for Export Facilitation scheme. (Page 27)

question: India and the European Union (EU) agreed to grant each other Most Favoured Nation (MFN) status. What is the duration for which this status is granted once the Free Trade Agreement (FTA) enters into force?
rationale: India and the European Union (EU) agreed to grant each other Most Favoured Nation (MFN) status for a period of five years. (Page 27)

question: Which country recently became the first in the Americas, and the second globally after Jordan, to eliminate leprosy as a public health problem?
rationale: The WHO officially verified that Chile has eliminated leprosy as a public health problem, making it the first country in the Americas and the second globally after Jordan. (Page 27)

question: The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) recently added Kuwait and Papua New Guinea to its 'Grey List'. Which of the following countries is currently under the 'Black List' (High-Jurisdictions subject to a Call for Action)?
rationale: FATF made no new changes to its Black List. The countries currently on the Black List are Iran, the Democratic People’s Republic of Korea (DPRK), and Myanmar. (Page 28)

question: Donald Trump announced the construction of the USA's first new oil refinery in 50 years. Which Indian company is heavily investing in this project located at the Port of Brownsville, Texas?
rationale: The USA will build its first new oil refinery in nearly 50 years with investment from Reliance Industries Limited (RIL), led by Mukesh Ambani. (Page 28)

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the visit of Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney to India:

1. It was his first official visit to India since his predecessor Justin Trudeau visited in 2018.
2. India set a bilateral trade goal of USD 100 billion by 2030 with Canada.
3. The leaders launched negotiations for the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA).

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: Statements 1 and 3 are correct. Statement 2 is incorrect because the bilateral trade goal set with Canada is USD 50 billion by 2030, not USD 100 billion. (Page 29)

question: During his visit to Israel, PM Narendra Modi became the first Indian Prime Minister to address the Israeli Parliament (Knesset) and was conferred an award. What was the name of the award?

options.A: Order of the State of Israel

rationale: PM Narendra Modi was conferred the 'Medal of the Knesset' by Knesset Speaker Amir Ohana, becoming the first Indian PM to receive this honour. (Page 30)

question: Alexander Stubb recently paid a four-day State Visit to India and attended the 11th Raisina Dialogue 2026 as the Chief Guest. He is the President of which country?
rationale: The President of Finland, Alexander Stubb, paid a four-day State Visit to India and attended the 11th Raisina Dialogue 2026 as the Chief Guest. (Pages 29-30)

question:
Match the following locations with the significant projects inaugurated/launched by PM Narendra Modi during his recent visits:

Column I:
1. Kayad, Ajmer (Rajasthan)
2. Sanand, Gujarat
3. Madurai, Tamil Nadu

Column II:
A. Semiconductor ATMP facility of Micron
B. Infrastructure projects worth Rs 4,400 crore
C. Nationwide HPV Vaccination Programme

Select the correct answer:
rationale: Kayad, Ajmer - Nationwide HPV Vaccination Programme (1-C); Sanand, Gujarat - Semiconductor ATMP facility of Micron (2-A); Madurai, TN - Infrastructure projects worth Rs 4,400 crore (3-B). (Page 29)

question: The 7th National Report (NR7) submitted by India to the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD) presents an assessment of progress toward the 2030 global biodiversity goals. These goals are aligned with which framework?

options.D: Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction

rationale: The 7th National Report (NR7) evaluates performance across 23 national biodiversity targets aligned with the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework (KMGBF). (Page 20)

question: The Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE) commemorated its 25th Foundation Day on March 1, 2026. Which portal was launched during this event to strengthen monitoring and facilitate compliance?
rationale: During the 25th Foundation Day of BEE, the Renewable Consumption Obligation (RCO) Portal was launched to strengthen monitoring and facilitate compliance. (Page 18)

question:
Arrange the following phases of the Mandatory Gold Hallmarking order in ascending order based on the number of districts covered in each phase:

1. Phase V
2. Phase II
3. Phase I
4. Phase VI

Select the correct answer:
rationale: Phase I covered 256 districts. Phase II expanded to 288. Phase V covered 373. Phase VI covered 380 districts. The ascending order is 3, 2, 1, 4. (Page 22)

question:
Assertion (A): The 'Gyan Bharatam National Manuscript Survey' aims to map India's manuscript heritage across the country.
Reason (R): It leverages the International Image Interoperability Framework (IIIF) to ensure standardised digital access for global scholars.

Choose the correct option:

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.
options.C: A is true but R is false.
options.D: A is false but R is true.
options.E: Both A and R are false.

rationale: Both Assertion and Reason are true facts mentioned in the document. However, the use of IIIF technology (Reason) is a feature to enable digital access, while the primary aim of mapping the heritage (Assertion) is a broader goal, making R a true statement but not the primary explanatory cause of A's existence. (Page 14)

question: India's first Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) Automated Teller Machine (ATM) was launched by Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL) in which city?
rationale: BPCL launched India's 1st LPG ATM at Central Park Flower Valley in Sector 33, Sohna, in Gurugram, Haryana. (Page 14)

question: Which among the following digital tools was NOT one of the four newly launched soft digital tools for Census-2027 by Union Minister Amit Shah?

options.D: Census Management and Monitoring System (CMMS) Portal

rationale: The four tools launched are HLBC Web App, HLO Mobile App, SE Portal, and CMMS Portal. The 'NPR Sync Portal' was not among them. (Page 13)

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'Janaushadhi Saptah 2026':

1. It was observed from 1 to 7 March 2026.
2. It promotes awareness about the affordability and quality of generic medicines under PMBJP.
3. The campaign concluded with the observance of the 10th Janaushadhi Diwas.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
rationale: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. Statement 3 is incorrect because the week-long campaign concluded with the observance of the 8th Janaushadhi Diwas (not 10th) on 7 March 2026. (Page 19)

question: Under the 6th phase of the mandatory hallmarking of Gold Jewellery, consumers can verify the authenticity and purity of hallmarked jewellery using the 'Verify HUID' feature. On which mobile application is this feature available?
rationale: Consumers can verify the authenticity and purity of hallmarked jewellery using the 'Verify HUID' feature on the BIS CARE mobile application. (Page 22)

question:
Match the following initiatives launched by Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw with their respective features:

Column I:
1. National AI Skilling Programme
2. MyWAVES
3. Advanced EPG & In-built Satellite Tuner

Column II:
A. Allows citizens to create, upload and share content
B. Enables watching DD Free Dish channels without a separate set-top box
C. Designed to train 15,000 participants from creative and media sectors

Select the correct answer:
rationale: National AI Skilling Programme - Train 15,000 participants (1-C); MyWAVES - Feature to create/share content (2-A); Advanced EPG & In-built Tuner - Watch DD Free Dish without STB (3-B). (Page 15)

question: Which of the following state governments presented a budget that specifically highlighted the allocation of Rs 260 crore for the distribution of domestic LPG cylinders on Holi and Diwali?
rationale: Delhi's CM Rekha Gupta presented the budget, and a key highlight was the allocation of Rs 260 crore for the distribution of domestic LPG cylinders on Holi and Diwali. (Page 9)
